hooks.text: Your bank says the transfer fee is waived. You still lost 300 dollars on it. Both things are true at the same time.
Banks have two ways to charge you on a wire. One shows up on the receipt. The other one you will never see.
Send 10,000 dollars to your supplier. The bank charges zero fee, then quietly keeps 300 in the exchange rate. No line item anywhere.
A business owner checked his wire receipt. Zero fee. He felt smart, until he compared the rate to Google and saw 300 dollars gone.
Every wire that says fee waived is still costing you 1 to 3 percent in the rate. On big payments that is hundreds, gone, every single time.
